title: abies alba subsp. borisii-regis
intro: Abies alba subsp. borisii-regis, also known as Bulgarian fir, is a subspecies of fir tree native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Bulgarian fir include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which are available in various forms, including capsules, tinctures, and topical creams.
